prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: total electronic energy of hydrogen peroxide (optimized geometry), units eV.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in xtb.
Geometry provenance: starting coordinates supplied by user (/Users/evane/.../hydrogen_peroxide.xyz); geometry relaxed in this run with the chemkit geometry-optimize skill (ASE BFGS optimizer, fmax target 0.05 eV Å⁻¹) until convergence in 17 steps (`converged: true`).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software/backend: xtb via chemkit engine (GFN2-xTB Hamiltonian).
Convergence & caveats: SCF and geometry optimization both converged; no warnings were returned by the engine. Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB; no basis set is involved.
Live output / reproducibility: the full stdout/stderr stream was written live to the calc-specific .out file in the caller’s working directory (announce path available on request), and the structured result JSON to /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpzp_o1_gy/live.json (contains cli_invocation, converged flag, energies, optimized .xyz). Optimized coordinates saved to /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/geometry-optimize-validation/hydrogen-peroxide/20260624-114623_hydrogen-peroxide_geometry_optimize/hydrogen_peroxide_xtb_opt.xyz.

Result: total energy = −246.38223 eV (GFN2-xTB, gas phase).
